_doc: Daily market-state snapshot. Written by the close-of-market run, read by the next morning run (and the next close run for day-over-day deltas). This is reasoning fuel — the reader never sees this file, only the conclusions drawn from it. Fields left null were not collected; collect them next run rather than guessing. Add fields freely as new data sources prove useful; note new sources in playbook.md.

spx.note: Opened 7,410.85, dipped to 7,363.01 in the first hour (under the 7,395 shelf, never near 7,300), then rallied THROUGH the 7,424 flip AND the literal 7,440 line to 7,456.40 before fading to close 7,431.46 — above the flip on both feeds, 9 points under the stale 7,440 quote. Range 1.26% (7,363→7,456) — NOT a quiet session despite the +0.5% headline; positive near-gamma slowed the move, didn't prevent it. First close above the flip since June 4. Week +0.65% (7,383.58 → 7,431.46) — first UP week since the 9-week streak snapped (June 1-5 was -2.6%). Record 7,599.96 (June 1) now -2.27% away. Resolved 2026-06-09-pm-gamma-reclaim PARTIAL (mechanism level reclaimed, quoted number missed).

dow.note: +353.51. SPCX debut day: opened $150, high $176.52, closed $160.95 (+19.2% vs $135 fixed offer), >$2T cap, ~$75B raised — largest IPO ever, absorbed with the index UP. Proxy unwind: RKLB -10.8% to $102.39 (despite Nasdaq-100 inclusion announced for June 22), SATS swung -10% to +4.1% close $133.40. ADBE -6.8% to $204.02 (3rd straight down day through a beat-and-raise; $233.38 line now -13.5% away).

regime.rationale: Upgraded from bull-under-stress per the criterion pre-stated in the June 11 snapshot ('close above the flip zone -> bull confirmed') — SPX closed 7,431.46, above the flip on both feeds (Polygon 7,406 / CBOE 7,424), after trading 7,456 intraday. The mechanical case: aggregate net GEX turned POSITIVE on both feeds (+$39.6B Polygon / +$17.1B CBOE) for the first time this drawdown — dealers now dampen moves at the index level; VIX crushed to 17.68 (lowest close since the June 5 selloff); first up week since the streak snapped; the $75B SPCX supply test passed with the index up; breadth fine (Dow led, small caps were the week's leaders). The honest caveats that keep this a watchful bull, not a victory lap: the entire peace repricing rests on an UNSIGNED deal (Tehran disputed the draft text today); BoJ + FOMC + triple-witching land inside the next five sessions; the put-side hedge floor thinned (big shelf expired today, next thick one at 7,200); and the toll-payer software complex keeps de-rating internally (ADBE).
regime.what_changes_it: Close back below the flip zone (7,406-7,424) -> back to bull-under-stress; the calm-tape call (2026-06-12-pinned-into-opex) dying on a wide-range session that started above the flip -> framework itself gets demoted. Close below 7,265 before June 17 FOMC -> 2026-06-11-no-revisit-7265 wrong AND the June 11-12 repricing was a trap. A weekend deal slip is the obvious catalyst for all of the above; per playbook lesson 1, the levels govern, the headline is context.

key_levels.notes: Support: 7420 = near-dated (<=2DTE) put shelf on both feeds — hedges cluster right under the close; 7395 = the old call wall, now first price-memory support; 7200 = aggregate put wall on BOTH feeds — NOTE the 7,300 shelf that held all week EXPIRED with today's options, so the floor below 7,395 is thin until new hedging stacks. Resistance: 7425-7430 = aggregate call wall zone (Polygon 7430 / CBOE 7425) — SPX closed 7,431.46 ON it; first fight Monday is immediate; 7456 = June 12 high (only price memory above); 7,599.96 = June 1 record (-2.27%).

gamma.source: polygon/computed (scripts/market_levels.py --audit, verdict: pass 9/9). CBOE crosscheck: flip 7424.3, call wall 7425 (0.07%), put wall 7200 (0.00%).
gamma.note: REGIME EVENT: aggregate net GEX positive on BOTH feeds (+$39.6B Polygon / +$17.1B CBOE) — first positive print of the cycle; near-view also positive. Dealers long gamma at the index level: rallies get sold into the 7,425-7,430 wall, dips get bought — pinning mechanics into June 19 triple-witching. Flip zone: feeds disagree 7,406 (Polygon) vs 7,424 (CBOE) — treat as a 7,406-7,424 zone; CBOE number used for the chart key because Polygon's stale spot (7,377.6 vs actual close 7,431.46) biases its flip iteration low. Put wall ROLLED DOWN 7,300 -> 7,200 on both feeds (this week's protective hedges expired today) — the downside is less defended than any night this week even as the regime turned calm; flag for the morning brief. Near-view shelves bracket the close tightly (7,420 put / 7,430 call) = Monday opens inside a pin.
gamma.caveat: Recurring Polygon spot-staleness artifact (spot 7,377.6 at the ~17:00 CT fetch vs 7,431.46 close; CBOE spot matched the close exactly). Walls derive from OI and agree across feeds — levels accepted; flip taken from CBOE. CBOE near-view sample tiny on a Friday evening (n=4 contracts <=2DTE) — Polygon near-view (n=489) used for the shelf keys.
gamma.audit_log: 2026-06-12 side-by-side day 4: script verdict pass 9/9. Fresh WebSearch snippet (InsiderFinance/FlashAlpha indexed): flip 7,425.85 vs CBOE computed 7,424.3 (0.02% — exact for practical purposes) vs Polygon 7,406 (0.27%); snippet put wall 7,275 sits between tonight's computed 7,200 and the expired 7,300 (snippet likely stale-blended); snippet call wall 8,000 is the recurring far-OTM max-OI artifact (computed 7,425-7,430 coheres with the tape — the close pinned to it). Tape-coherence: pass. Polygon stock closes returned as_of 2026-06-11 at the 17:00 CT run (known free-tier staleness) — Yahoo chart API used for all 13 same-day closes, all stamped 16:00 ET June 12; SPYx10 = 7,417.5 vs SPX 7,431.46 (ratio 10.02, consistent). Data-integrity flag: Yahoo returned MAGS close == prev close (64.91 exactly, unchanged day) — plausible for a flat Mag-7 day but verify Monday.

rates_commodities.note: 10Y ~4.47% (hovering, per TradingEconomics — bond market idling into BoJ+FOMC week). WTI: settled BELOW $85, down 3%+, an EIGHT-WEEK LOW (CNBC: 'crude falls below $85 as US and Iran near a deal'; exact settle not confirmed in any source tonight — left null rather than guessed; morning run should backfill). The decline came THROUGH Tehran publicly disputing the leaked draft text — third straight session of oil grading war headlines as noise, the strongest run of support yet for the oil-is-the-tell hypothesis (grades Tuesday June 16). Brent/gold not collected tonight.

volatility_flow.vix_note: VIX 19.44 -> 17.68 (-9.1%), low 17.59 — lowest close since the June 5 selloff began, below the entire June 5-12 stress range. Intraday search snippets showed 19.01 early-session; Yahoo ^VIX API close used (API beats snippets). Fear gauge and dealer positioning now agree: both calm — the full inverse of June 10's configuration.
volatility_flow.put_call_note: Not collected (lags 1-2 days in free sources).
volatility_flow.note: No corroborated large-print flow found today. SPCX day-one options began trading; no clean OI data yet — worth a look next week as a sentiment read on the IPO pipeline.

technicals.breadth_note: Dow led (+0.7%) over Nasdaq (+0.31%) — a rotation-flavored up day rather than a tech melt-up; SPCX +19% absorbed without draining the tape. Space proxies were the red pocket (RKLB -10.8%, SATS intraday -10% before recovering). Memory cooled: MU -1.4% to $981.61 (faded from $1,012 high) on the prime-broker leverage-tightening reports out of Korea; AVGO -0.9%. CRWV +5.0% to $100.55 — reclaimed $100 two days after pricing the 9.625% notes.
technicals.note: DMAs estimated by drift from June 8 anchors — refresh from a direct reading early next week (carried 3 sessions now).

calendar.event: US-Iran signing watch (weekend, possibly Europe; admin official: 80% in 'coming days')
calendar.why: Oil below $85 and the whole gamma unclench are priced on the signature. Trump denied the Iranian-leaked text; Tehran says no draft approved. Binary in both directions; the 7,200 put wall is the thin floor if it slips.
Three straight confirmations including the hardest tests (declared Hormuz closure -> oil -4%; disputed deal text -> oil -3%). Leaning promote to playbook Active.
The sleeper: a hike squeezes yen-funded leverage under global tech, the same week prime brokers are already tightening the Korea memory trade. Watch USD/JPY.

Hold expected (2026-06-10-fomc-hold-hawkish live: hold + December-hike odds staying >50%). Hawks hold supercore +0.55% and core PPI +0.4%; doves hold crude at 8-week lows. Also the horizon for 2026-06-11-no-revisit-7265 (no close below 7,265 before the statement).

Quarter's biggest expiry, arriving with dealers LONG gamma — should cement the pin. Our calm-tape call grades against it: any session starting above the flip printing a 1.5%+ range kills it. Also ADBE $233.38 software-derate line grades at Friday's close.

calendar.event: MRVL + FLEX join S&P 500; RKLB joins Nasdaq-100
calendar.why: Today's proxy unwind (RKLB -10.8% ON inclusion news) supports the sell-the-news read; 2026-06-09-mrvl-inclusion-bid ($250 line) all but decided — formal grade on the date.

calendar.event: MU fiscal Q3 earnings (approx — confirm date)
calendar.why: Guide $33.5B / 81% GM / $19.15 EPS; Wolfe's $1,250 PT math meets the income statement with prime brokers tightening the crowd's leverage into the print.
Fed's preferred gauge; the supercore echo check, post-FOMC.
No SPCX close below $135 through June 30; a broken IPO re-arms the supply fear for the September OpenAI window.

open_questions: ANSWERED 2026-06-12 (from June 11): the 7,395 call wall did NOT cap the reclaim — SPX punched through 7,395 AND the 7,424 flip, traded 7,456.40, closed 7,431.46. pm-gamma-reclaim graded PARTIAL (above the mechanism's level, below the stale 7,440 quote; lesson 7 added to playbook).

ANSWERED 2026-06-12 (from June 11): ADBE did NOT hold the after-hours level once digested — two downgrades + the CFO exit took it down another 6.8% to $204.02, third straight down session through a beat-and-raise. The software de-rate has momentum, not just a personnel excuse; $233.38 reclaim by June 19 now needs +14.4%.

ANSWERED 2026-06-12 (from June 11): the CRWV 9.625% coupon is being read as ACCESS, not distress — stock +5.0% to $100.55 two days post-pricing, reclaiming $100. The financing-access bear case is quiet for the quarter barring a new shock.

ANSWERED 2026-06-12 (from June 11, partially): the deal did NOT sign by Friday but didn't slip either — Trump denied the leaked text while his administration briefed 80% odds 'in coming days'; Araghchi: 'never been closer.' Oil graded the dispute as noise (-3%). The weekend question stands.

CARRIED (from June 10, now urgent): supercore decomposition — BLS detail tables (recreation, medical, insurance m/m) STILL unchecked, and the FOMC is Wednesday. Settle it in the Monday run or drop the thread honestly.

NEW: the put-side floor thinned exactly as the regime calmed (7,300 shelf expired; next thick wall 7,200; near shelf only 7,420). If the weekend deal slips, does the positive-gamma cushion hold the tape above 7,395, or does thin OI below mean a faster trip to 7,265 than the calm suggests?

NEW: SPCX day two — does the float (fixed-price, no underwriter peg, retail-heavy day one per coverage) hold $150+ once the debut adrenaline fades, and when do lockup/follow-on mechanics first get priced?

NEW: MU faded $1,012 -> $981.61 on the Korea prime-broker margin-tightening reports. Is the leverage clamp a one-day story or the start of a positioning unwind into the June 24 print? (Watch SK Hynix/Samsung Monday session.)
NEW: MAGS printed close == prev close (64.91) on Yahoo — verify Monday that this wasn't a data artifact.
